video.title: Can Sprinters Survive Tough Climb? | Giro D'Italia 2022 Stage 5 Highlights

transcript.clean_text: stage four of the jitter italia had taken the riders to etna leonard kemna attacking from a bunch of 14 riders trying to make it up to juanpe lopes with the most advanced of all of them lopez messed up on the final corner on the approach to the mighty mountain leonard kemner winning the stage lopez would take the pink jersey into stage five on stage five it now was again in the background it started from catania the fifth stage of the judo italia the second and final day in sicily and linking two of its biggest three cities catania with messina most of the stage almost a repeat of one we'd had a couple of years earlier finishing in villafranca tirena and won by arnold de mar this time 38 kilometers extra around the coastline finishing in the hometown of vincenzo nebali in messina 174 kilometers for stage five of this year's gino the new malaya rosa juan pelope is the first spaniard in seven years to pull on the pink jersey it took around four kilometers for the day's break to form again we had two riders from drawn up anthroniocately and the same two members philippe potaliani and matthias when it came to the intermediate sprint early on at the bottom of the day's only climb taliani was the man to win that sprint he do so ahead of jakob henan the finnish rider for asher dazer citroen also in the break where my history of eulo up there too pardiani represented in the breakaway with tony only three points are available in the hunt for the malaya chiclamino behind and they were taken by binyam gilmai no resistance there but machiavelli sent his team to work on the climb for telemandarazi a 20 kilometer climb at four percent alpacin fenix trying to drop pure sprinters caveadish and ewen and it didn't take too long for them to succeed cavendish the first spotted by the cameras but by this stage ewen was already further back they were already drifting towards a minute behind the peloton with around 10 k's to go on the climb ewen was in real trouble though having just one man with him to help out not seemingly on a good day closer to the top arnold de mayo was dropped as well he had a good group of riders around him and they were doing the best to keep him in touch the breakaway would just about take the king of the mountains points my s3 taking most of them ahead of base and 48 seconds with the gap over the top the break would be caught with 66 kilometers to go israel premier tech trying to pull things along for the likes of nidzola who was still in the group fernando gaviria was still there cavendish wasn't getting back on however with you and a further two minutes behind alma dimar did make it back and straight away he sent his team to work at the front to make sure that cavendish and ewan would struggle to retain or gain any contact inside 50 kilometers to go approaching the intermediate sprint and this one for bonus seconds as well as intermediate sprint points ben swift rather cleverly taking the three seconds away of joao almeida who nevertheless got two he chipping two back on the rest of his gc rivals no cavendish no ewan confirmed 11 kilometers to go and things really started hotting up inside the final three kilometers a few twists and turns taken on by borah hansgroh and the rest of the gc teams looking to protect their leaders going under the flam rouge though and approaching the most difficult turn it would be group deji's miles scotson to win the race for the corner his leader arnold demar had been caught out a little two or three wheels back but he wasn't too badly placed ramon singledom returned him right to the wheel but there would be competition downhill for two or three hundred meters but then a headwind hit single damn leading out demar eventually coming off his wheel with several competitors alongside him gilmar was boxed into one side bar house was just around him consonant was there as well gavidia was gaining nizora was up there but it would be arnold who would win once again demar back with his first victory of the season and back in sicily we'd enjoyed his last visit two years previous tomorrow ahead of gabiria and zola with ballerini taking quick step alpha finals best position germai in another top five with bajas dynasty test fazion turns and consonny all making the top ten machiafonda pool denied an opportunity with a rider coming just in front of him he would not contest the sprint in the end that meant that for the pool would be just outside the positions needed to score points and almo dimar would score enough points to take him into the lead in the maya chiclamino points competition binyam kemai would be second fernando gabridia third found a pool down to fourth a big day then for the frenchman a very big day for group f who came to this giro with the aim of a stage win they now have one juanpe lopes was still living the dream as the leader of the giro d'italia the general classification on changed apart from those two seconds in seventh place they're going to almeida lopez the first spaniard since alberto contador seven years previous to where the mallarosa and the young man from trek segafredo will now take it to the mainland with stage six expected to be worn for the sprinters again evers are slightly longer this time up the calabrian coast a two-star stage according to the organizers finishing in scalia on the riviera de cedric the only mountain difficulty coming in the first quarter of a stage and you can watch the jittered italia each and every day live and on demand it's gcn plus discovery plus and
